term_json: {"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition: "},{"type":"text","text":"The repetition of consonant sounds, particularly at the beginnings of words."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Example:"},{"type":"text","text":" "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"\"Pensive poets\" or \"nattering nabobs of negativism\"."}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition:"},{"type":"text","text":" Repetition of similar vowel sounds."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Example:"},{"type":"text","text":" \""},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"free as a breeze"},{"type":"text","text":"\" or \""},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"slowly the boat goes"},{"type":"text","text":"\""}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition:"},{"type":"text","text":" Repetition of consonant sounds within or at the end of words."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Example: "},{"type":"text","text":"\""},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"pitter patter"},{"type":"text","text":"\" or \""},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"Mike likes his new bike"},{"type":"text","text":"\""}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition:"},{"type":"text","text":" Two consecutive, rhyming lines."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Example: "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"\"For thy sweet love remember'd such wealth brings / That then I scorn than change my state with kings\" (The Bard)"}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition:"},{"type":"text","text":" The continuation of a sentence without a pause beyond the end of a line or stanza."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Examples:"},{"type":"text","text":" Look for verse lines without any ending punctuation to indicate a pause is needed. "}]}]}
{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition:"},{"type":"text","text":" Poetry without a regular meter or rhyme scheme."}]}]}
{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition:"},{"type":"text","text":" Poetry without a regular meter or rhyme scheme."}]}]}
{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition:"},{"type":"text","text":" Poetry without a regular meter or rhyme scheme."}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition:"},{"type":"text","text":" Exaggerated statements not meant to be taken literally."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Examples: "},{"type":"text","text":"\""},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"I've told you a million times,\""},{"type":"text","text":" or "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"\"That bag weighs a ton\"."}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition:"},{"type":"text","text":" Vivid descriptive language that appeals to the senses."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Examples: "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"\"The golden sun, a blazing ball of fire, sank below the horizon\""},{"type":"text","text":" or "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"\"The tops of trees that are all aquiver with new spring life\""}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition:"},{"type":"text","text":" A direct comparison between two unlike things."}]},{"type":"paragraph"},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Examples:"},{"type":"text","text":" "}]},{"type":"bulletList","content":[{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"\""},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"The fog comes / on little cat feet."},{"type":"text","text":"\" ("},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Sandburg"},{"type":"text","text":") "}]}]},{"type":"listItem","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"\""},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"All the world's a stage, / And all the men and women merely players."},{"type":"text","text":"\" ("},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Shakespeare"},{"type":"text","text":")"}]}]}]},{"type":"paragraph"}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition:"},{"type":"text","text":" The structured, rhythmic pattern of stressed and unstressed syllables."}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition:"},{"type":"text","text":" Words that imitate the sound they describe. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Example:"},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":" \"Buzz, slurp, bang, boom\""}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition:"},{"type":"text","text":" A line or set of lines repeated at intervals."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Examples:"},{"type":"text","text":" Villanelles like Thomas's \""},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"Do not go gentle into that good night"},{"type":"text","text":"\" or Poe's \""},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"Nevermore"},{"type":"text","text":"\" in the Raven"}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition:"},{"type":"text","text":" A comparison using \"like\" or \"as\"."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Examples: "},{"type":"text","text":"\""},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"Does it dry up / like a raisin in the sun?"},{"type":"text","text":"\" (Langston Hughes)"}]}]}
{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition:"},{"type":"text","text":" A grouped set of lines, often separated by spaces."}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Definition: A 14-line poem, often in iambic pentameter with a specific rhyme scheme "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Examples: \""},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"Shall I compare thee to a summer's day?"},{"type":"text","text":"\" ("},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://www.poetryfoundation.org/poems/45087/sonnet-18-shall-i-compare-thee-to-a-summers-day","target":null,"rel":null,"class":null,"title":null}},{"type":"italic"}],"text":"see Shakespeare"},{"type":"text","text":")"}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition: "},{"type":"text","text":"Four-line stanza, or unit of four lines of verse, rhymed or unrhymed."}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Example: "},{"type":"text","text":"(Robert Frost)"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"The woods are lovely, dark and deep,"},{"type":"hardBreak","marks":[{"type":"italic"}]},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"But I have promises to keep,"},{"type":"hardBreak","marks":[{"type":"italic"}]},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"And miles to go before I sleep,"},{"type":"hardBreak","marks":[{"type":"italic"}]},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"text":"And miles to go before I sleep."}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition:"},{"type":"text","text":" Three-line stanza, or unit of three lines of verse, rhymed or unrhymed. "}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Examples:"},{"type":"text","text":" See the "},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://www.poetryfoundation.org/education/glossary/tercet","target":null,"rel":null,"class":null,"title":null}},{"type":"italic"}],"text":"Poetry Foundation"},{"type":"text","text":" for a complete explanation using different rhyme schemes"}]}]}

{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Definition"},{"type":"text","text":": The villanelle is a highly structured poem made up of five tercets followed by a quatrain, with two repeating rhymes and two refrains. ("},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://poets.org/glossary/villanelle","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":null,"title":null}},{"type":"bold"}],"text":"See more on poets.org"},{"type":"text","text":")"}]},{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"text":"Examples"},{"type":"text","text":": Dylan Thomas: \""},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://www.poetryfoundation.org/poems/46569/do-not-go-gentle-into-that-good-night","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":null,"title":null}},{"type":"italic"}],"text":"Do Not Go Gentle Into That Night"},{"type":"text","text":"\" or Elizabeth Bishop: \""},{"type":"text","marks":[{"type":"link","attrs":{"href":"https://www.poetryfoundation.org/poems/47536/one-art","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener noreferrer nofollow","class":null,"title":null}}],"text":"One Art"},{"type":"text","text":"\""}]}]}
